# Allergy & Asthma Care of Waco

Board-certified allergists and immunologists serving Waco and Central Texas since 1978.

## What We Do

We diagnose and treat allergies, asthma, and immune system conditions using the most current evidence-based protocols. We are not a corporate chain — we are a local, independent practice with deep roots in the Waco community.

## Location

221 Jewell Drive, Waco, TX 76712
Phone: (254) 751-0795
Hours: Monday–Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM

## Specialties

- Immunotherapy — allergy shots and sublingual immunotherapy (allergy drops)
- Allergy testing — skin prick testing, patch testing, intradermal testing
- Asthma diagnosis and long-term management
- Pediatric allergy and asthma care (all ages, including infants)
- Cedar fever / Mountain Cedar pollen (peaks December–February in Central Texas)
- Grass pollen allergy (peaks April–June in Central Texas)
- Weed pollen allergy (peaks August–November)
- Mold allergy
- Dust mite allergy
- Food allergy testing and management
- Insect venom allergy (bee stings)
- Drug allergy evaluation

## Conditions Treated

Allergic rhinitis (hay fever), allergic asthma, atopic dermatitis (eczema), food allergies, anaphylaxis, insect venom allergy, drug allergies, sinusitis related to allergies, urticaria (hives), angioedema.

## Key Facts

- Founded: 1978
- Patients served: 10,000+ families
- Physicians: Board-certified in Allergy & Immunology by the American Board of Allergy and Immunology (ABAI)
- Location: Waco, Texas — serving Waco, Hewitt, Woodway, Lorena, Temple, Killeen, and surrounding Central Texas communities

## About Immunotherapy

Immunotherapy — commonly called allergy shots — is the only treatment that changes how the immune system responds to allergens, rather than simply masking symptoms. Patients receive gradually increasing doses of their specific allergens over a build-up phase (typically 6–12 months), then transition to monthly maintenance shots. Clinical studies show that 85% of patients experience significant, long-lasting symptom reduction. Immunotherapy can also prevent the development of new allergies and reduce the risk of asthma in children with allergic rhinitis.

## Central Texas Allergy Context

Waco sits in one of the highest Mountain Cedar (Ashe juniper) pollen zones in the United States. Cedar fever — a misnomer, as it causes no fever — typically runs from late November through February and can produce severe symptoms even in people who have never had allergies before. Oak, elm, pecan, and ash pollens follow in spring. Grass season peaks in May and June. Ragweed and other weeds dominate fall. Year-round exposures include dust mites, mold, and animal dander.
